revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:五百井神社]] {{Expand Japanese|五百井神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ionoi Shrine | native_name = 五百井神社 | image = Ionoi shrine 2020-11 Torii.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ionoi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.011586|136.001387}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ki no mata no kami|lt=Ki no mata no kami|ja|木俣神|zh|木俁神|qid=Q11092833|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ionoi Shrine'''|五百井神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province|ja|近江国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11638477|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 8 ** P155: {{ill|Q11462117|ltq=Q11462117|qid=Q11462117|1=Ogura Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Ifukino Shrine|lt=Ifukino Shrine|qid=Q135039792|1=Ifukino Shrine (Q135039792)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q857310|ltq=Q857310|qid=Q857310|1=Rittō, Shiga}} * {{ill|Q843882|ltq=Q843882|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} * {{ill|Kurita District, Shiga|lt=Kurita district|en|Kurita District, Shiga|ja|栗太郡|zh|栗太郡|qid=Q6446084|1=Kurita District, Shiga}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.011586|136.001387}} == image (P18) == * Ionoi shrine 2020-11 Torii.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q11092833|ltq=Q11092833|qid=Q11092833|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181788 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1160005008237 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県栗東市下戸山20', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:五百井神社]] {{wikidata link|Q22117831}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ioinoi Shrine |Image = [[File:Ionoi shrine 2020-11 Torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ritto City|Q857310|qid=Q857310|1=Rittō, Shiga}} Shimotoyama 20 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|Q11092833|qid=Q11092833|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Other names = Ashii Shrine |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|qid=Q2550|1=May 5}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ioinoi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ioinoi Shrine''' (いおのいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shimotoyama, {{ill|lt=Ritto City|Q857310|qid=Q857310|1=Rittō, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The {{ill|lt=shrine crest|Q1067875|qid=Q1067875|1=Chartrand}} is the “descending wisteria” motif.[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=369 Ioinoi Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines). === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|Q11092833|qid=Q11092833|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} – also known as “Mii-no-Kami.” A deity statue dating from the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}} (designated a Tangible Cultural Property of Shiga Prefecture) has been handed down. === History === === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. The shrine precincts are located on the southwestern foothills of Mount Anyōji; on the summit stand the inner shrine (Sannō Shrine) and the Ryūō Shrine. This inner shrine is said to be the place where the enshrined deity descended.{{Sfn|Ioinoi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} There is also a tradition that the shrine was worshipped by the Ashii clan. In the late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} anthology *Fubokushō*, the Kanasatsu River flowing in front of the shrine precincts appears as “Ihonoi River,” leading to a theory that this shrine was worshipped as a guardian deity of the irrigation well supplying water there.{{Sfn|Ioinoi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} === Overview === According to shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|qid=Q29966|1=851}}), in January, the shrine was granted the divine rank of Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade.{{Sfn|Ioinoi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} According to the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|qid=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}*, a simultaneous promotion of various deities to Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade took place on January 27 of that year. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Register of Deities|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in the fifth year of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed as “Ashii Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Kurita District|Q6446084|qid=Q6446084|1=Kurita District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, and is included among the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}.{{Sfn|Ioinoi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} In manuscript copies of this register, the reading of “Ashii” is glossed as “Ihonoino.” As mentioned above, in the late Kamakura period *Fubokushō* the name “Ihonoi River” appears, and it is said that from around the end of the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} the shrine name came to be written as “Ioinoi.”{{Sfn|Ioinoi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} In the fifth year of {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|qid=Q1327259|1=Ansei}} ({{ill|lt=1858|Q7684|qid=Q7684|1=1858}}), the main hall was rebuilt,{{Sfn|Ioinoi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} but this main hall (a one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure) has since been lost due to typhoon damage described below. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|qid=Q7766|1=1876}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In September {{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|qid=Q1998|1=2013}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 25), a landslide occurred on the slope behind the shrine precincts due to the effects of {{ill|lt=Typhoon No. 18 of 2013|Q14941852|qid=Q14941852|1=Typhoon Man-yi (2013)}}, and the shrine buildings, including the main hall and worship hall, were almost completely destroyed. As a result, the divine spirit was transferred to a {{ill|lt=temporary shrine|Q11489117|qid=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}, and rituals have continued there (as of May 2014). The deity statue that had been enshrined in the main hall was excavated from the soil two weeks after the landslide; later its artistic value was reappraised, and in {{ill|lt=2015|Q2002|qid=Q2002|1=2015}} (Heisei 27) it was designated a Tangible Cultural Property of Shiga Prefecture.[https://www.sankei.com/article/20140506-6OMGSUKO6FJEXCU5R4BG3B6XCM/ “Ritto’s typhoon-damaged Ioinoi Shrine ‘Grand Festival’ Shiga”] (Sankei News, May 6, 2014).
[http://kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20151221000044 “A strange fate: damaged deity statue becomes cultural property – buried by landslide, re-evaluated –”] (Kyoto Shimbun, December 21, 2015).
[http://mainichi.jp/articles/20151222/ddl/k25/040/545000c “Nine new prefectural cultural properties: Takashima’s Ninigi Shrine and Ritto’s wooden seated male deity, etc. / Shiga”] (Mainichi Shimbun, December 22, 2015). === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Hachiman Shrine === Cultural properties === === Shiga Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden seated male deity statue, 1 figure (sculpture) – Heian period work. Made in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ichiboku-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} technique, with a height of 33.8 centimeters. Designated on December 18, 2015 (Heisei 27).[http://www.city.ritto.shiga.jp/hakubutsukan/sub315.htm Shiga Prefecture designated cultural properties located in Ritto City] (Ritto City website).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/files/03dansinzazou.pdf Sculpture section]}} (Shiga Prefectural Board of Education website). ** Black-lacquered, gilt-bronze-mounted creeping oak-pattern saddle (craft) – Kamakura period work. Designated as a city cultural property in 1996 (Heisei 8) on December 6 under the name “Wooden creeping oak-pattern saddle,”[http://www.city.ritto.shiga.jp/hakubutsukan/sub216.htm Ritto City designated cultural properties] (Ritto City website). and designated as a prefectural cultural property on December 24, 2019 (Reiwa 1).{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/5147872.pdf Shiga Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 8, December 24, Reiwa 1]}} (Shiga Prefecture website). === Notes === {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=Ioinoi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 25: Place Names of Shiga Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|Ioinoi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=369 Ioinoi Shrine] – Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ionoi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ionoi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Rittō]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ritto, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]
